businessSolution: Every product needs a home. Creating well-organised categories is the first step to a browsable catalogue. A clear category structure helps customers find what they want faster, increases conversion, and makes your storefront look professional.
navigation: Product Management → Categories → Add New → Enter name in Default/EN/AR → Upload 1:1 image → Save
feature: Add a new product category with a name and a 1:1 ratio image in multiple languages (Default, English, Arabic)
outcome: The category is created and appears in the master category list, enabling products to be assigned to it
setupComments: Multilingual name fields (Default / EN / AR) and image upload required

businessSolution: Regularly reviewing your category structure ensures your catalogue stays organised as your product range grows. Exporting the list helps your merchandising team audit and plan category improvements offline.
navigation: Product Management → Categories → Use Search bar to find → Click Export to download as Excel
feature: View the full category list with search and export controls
outcome: I can quickly locate any category and export the list to Excel for audit purposes

businessSolution: Keeping category names and images accurate ensures customers aren't confused by outdated or misspelled labels. Correcting a category name instantly updates how it appears across the entire storefront.
navigation: Product Management → Categories → Click Edit icon next to category → Update name/image → Save
feature: Edit an existing category's name or image
outcome: Category information stays accurate without having to delete and recreate entries

businessSolution: Seasonal or underperforming categories can be hidden temporarily without losing any data. This lets you control what customers see based on business cycles — e.g., hide 'Winter Wear' during summer and re-enable it later.
navigation: Product Management → Categories → Toggle the Status switch to Enable/Disable
feature: Enable or disable a category using a status toggle
outcome: Hidden categories stop appearing in all product assignment dropdowns and customer storefronts instantly

businessSolution: Removing unused or test categories keeps your catalogue clean and prevents confusion for staff assigning products. The system protects you by only allowing deletion when no products are linked.
navigation: Product Management → Categories → Click Delete icon → Confirm deletion (only works if no products linked)
feature: Delete a category that has no linked products
outcome: Stale or test categories are removed, keeping the catalogue clean

businessSolution: Sub-categories provide a second level of classification (e.g., 'Cheese' under 'Dairy') that helps customers drill down into exactly what they need. A well-structured hierarchy improves shopability and search results.
navigation: Product Management → Sub Categories → Add New → Select parent category → Enter name in all languages → Save
feature: Add a new sub-category by selecting a parent main category and entering a name in all supported languages
outcome: Products can be nested under a main category for finer classification, improving shopability
setupComments: Parent category must exist before creating sub-category

businessSolution: Verifying parent-child relationships ensures products appear in the right section. A misclassified sub-category means customers won't find products where they expect them.
navigation: Product Management → Sub Categories → Review the list showing each sub-category alongside its parent
feature: View the sub-category list showing each sub-category alongside its parent category
outcome: I can verify the correct parent-child relationships at a glance

businessSolution: If a sub-category was accidentally placed under the wrong parent, reassigning it fixes the catalogue hierarchy without losing any linked products or historical data.
navigation: Product Management → Sub Categories → Click Edit → Change parent category or name → Save
feature: Edit a sub-category's name or reassign it to a different parent category
outcome: Mis-classified sub-categories can be corrected without data loss

businessSolution: Using the official template prevents formatting errors during bulk uploads. It ensures every column header matches exactly what the system expects, saving hours of troubleshooting failed imports.
navigation: Product Management → Bulk Import → Download Template (with or without current data)
feature: Download the official category bulk-import Excel template (with or without current data)
outcome: I have a correctly formatted file to fill in, preventing import errors
setupComments: Template must be .xls or .xlsx format

businessSolution: Bulk importing categories is essential when onboarding large product ranges or migrating from another platform. What would take days of manual data entry is completed in minutes.
navigation: Product Management → Bulk Import → Select 'Upload New Data' → Choose filled Excel file → Upload
feature: Upload a completed Excel file to import multiple new categories at once
outcome: Onboarding large category structures is done in minutes instead of hours of manual entry
setupComments: Upload New Data option selected

businessSolution: Mass corrections to category names, images, or status are applied in one operation. This is far more efficient than editing each record individually, especially when dealing with hundreds of categories.
navigation: Product Management → Bulk Import → Select 'Update Existing Data' → Choose corrected Excel → Upload
feature: Upload a completed Excel file to update existing category records in bulk
outcome: Mass corrections to names, images, or status are applied without editing each record individually
setupComments: Update Existing Data option selected

businessSolution: Exporting your category structure creates an offline backup and gives you a starting-point file for preparing your next bulk import or sharing with external teams for review.
navigation: Product Management → Bulk Export → Select sort/filter options → Click Export → Download Excel
feature: Export the full category and sub-category list as an Excel file with optional sort/filter options
outcome: I have an offline backup or a starting-point file for preparing the next bulk import

businessSolution: Product attributes like Colour, Material, or Finish enrich customer-facing detail pages with searchable and filterable characteristics. Customers can narrow down choices without scrolling through irrelevant items.
navigation: Product Management → Attributes → Add New → Enter attribute name in all languages → Save
feature: Add a new product attribute (e.g., Colour, Material, Finish) with multilingual name support
outcome: Descriptive characteristics can be attached to products, enriching customer-facing detail pages

businessSolution: As product lines evolve, outdated attributes clutter product forms. Regularly cleaning up the attribute dictionary keeps forms relevant and helps maintain data quality across your catalogue.
navigation: Product Management → Attributes → View list → Click Edit to update or Delete to remove
feature: View all attributes in a list and edit or delete individual attributes
outcome: The attribute dictionary stays current as product lines evolve

businessSolution: Correct units of measurement prevent customer confusion — selling rice 'per kg' vs 'per piece' makes a huge difference in pricing perception and order accuracy.
navigation: Product Management → Units → Add New → Enter unit name (e.g., kg, piece, litre) in all languages → Save
feature: Add a new unit of measurement (e.g., kg, piece, metre, litre) with multilingual name support
outcome: Products can specify the correct selling unit, avoiding customer confusion

businessSolution: Duplicate or outdated units (e.g., both 'KG' and 'Kilogram') create inconsistency in your product forms. Cleaning these up ensures only valid, standardised options appear for vendors.
navigation: Product Management → Units → View list → Edit to rename or Delete duplicates
feature: View the units list and edit or delete entries
outcome: Outdated or duplicate units are removed, ensuring only valid options appear in product forms

businessSolution: Associating products with recognisable brands builds customer trust and enables brand-based filtering on the storefront. Customers who search by brand can find their preferred items instantly.
navigation: Product Management → Brands → Add New → Enter brand name → Upload logo → Save
feature: Add a new brand with a name and an uploaded brand logo
outcome: Products can be associated with a recognisable brand, building customer trust

businessSolution: When you stop carrying a brand, disabling it hides it from all product forms and customer filters without losing historical sales data. Re-enable it if you resume the brand later.
navigation: Product Management → Brands → Toggle Status switch to Enable/Disable
feature: Enable or disable a brand using a status toggle
outcome: Discontinued brands are hidden from product forms without losing historical data

businessSolution: As your catalogue grows to hundreds of brands, the search function becomes essential for quickly locating any brand without scrolling through long lists.
navigation: Product Management → Brands → Use Search bar to find specific brands
feature: View and search the full brand list
outcome: I can quickly find any brand in a growing catalogue without scrolling

businessSolution: Full localisation of product names and descriptions ensures every customer — regardless of their language preference — sees product information they can understand. This directly impacts buying confidence.
navigation: Product Setup → Add New → Fill Name, Short Description, Long Description in Default/EN/AR tabs
feature: Enter product name, short description, and long description in Default, English, and Arabic
outcome: The product page is fully localised for all supported customer markets
setupComments: All three language tabs should be completed

businessSolution: High-quality product images and thumbnails are the most influential factor in online buying decisions. A missing thumbnail creates a blank space in product grids, making your store look unprofessional.
navigation: Product Setup → Add New → Upload Product Image (1:1 ratio) → Upload Thumbnail (mandatory)
feature: Upload a full-size 1:1 product image and a required thumbnail image
outcome: Customers see a high-quality visual and the thumbnail renders correctly in listing grids
setupComments: Thumbnail is mandatory; full image is recommended

businessSolution: Correct store, category, sub-category, and brand assignments determine where your product appears. Wrong assignments mean customers can't find the product through browsing, category pages, or brand filters.
navigation: Product Setup → Add New → Select Store → Select Category → Select Sub-Category → Select Brand
feature: Assign the product to a store, category, sub-category, and brand
outcome: The product appears in the correct storefront, category page, and brand filter
setupComments: Store and Category fields are mandatory

businessSolution: Setting purchase limits prevents hoarding during high-demand periods (e.g., max 5 hand sanitisers per order). The correct unit of measurement ensures customers know exactly what quantity they're buying.
navigation: Product Setup → Add New → Select Unit of Measurement → Set Max Purchase Quantity
feature: Select the unit of measurement and set a maximum purchase quantity limit per order
outcome: Customers buy the correct measured unit and bulk purchases beyond the limit are blocked at checkout

businessSolution: Accurate pricing, stock counts, and discount percentages drive purchase decisions. The storefront automatically calculates and displays the discounted price alongside the original, creating a compelling value proposition.
navigation: Product Setup → Add New → Enter Price → Enter Stock Quantity → Set Discount Percentage
feature: Set the product price, current stock quantity, and a discount percentage
outcome: The price, discounted price, and remaining stock are displayed accurately on the customer app

businessSolution: Product attributes (colour, size, material) become filterable tags on the storefront. Customers shopping for a 'Red, Cotton, Large' t-shirt can find it instantly without browsing the entire catalogue.
navigation: Product Setup → Add New → Scroll to Attributes section → Add attribute values
feature: Add product attributes such as colour, material, and size
outcome: Customers can filter and search by specific product characteristics

businessSolution: Reusing existing gallery assets prevents duplicate uploads and keeps your media storage organised. It also ensures visual consistency when the same product image is needed across multiple listings.
navigation: Product Setup → Add New → Click 'Add Info From Gallery' → Select existing image → Apply
feature: Import product information from the platform gallery to pre-fill image fields
outcome: Reusing existing gallery assets avoids duplicate uploads and speeds up listing
setupComments: Add Info From Gallery feature

businessSolution: Saving products as drafts allows your team to prepare listings in advance, review content for accuracy, and get internal approval before making anything visible to customers.
navigation: Product Setup → Add New → Fill all details → Click 'Save as Draft'
feature: Save the product as Draft without publishing it to the storefront
outcome: I can prepare a product in advance and review it before making it live

businessSolution: Publishing makes a product immediately visible and purchasable by customers. If admin approval is required, the product enters a review queue first, maintaining quality control over what reaches the storefront.
navigation: Product Setup → Add New → Fill all details → Click 'Publish' (may require admin approval)
feature: Publish the product to make it live and visible to customers immediately
outcome: New products become available for purchase without any additional admin steps
setupComments: Requires admin approval if 'Need Approval For Products' is enabled

businessSolution: The product list is your daily command centre for catalogue management. Efficient search, filtering, and pagination let you manage thousands of SKUs without losing track of any item.
navigation: Product Management → Product List → Use Search/Filter by category or status → Paginate
feature: View the complete product list with search, filter by category/status, and paginate results
outcome: I can find any product instantly even with thousands of SKUs

businessSolution: Quick inline editing from the list view saves time when making minor corrections. You don't need to navigate through multiple screens — spot an error, fix it, and move on.
navigation: Product Management → Product List → Click Edit icon next to product → Make changes → Save
feature: Edit any field of an existing product directly from the list view
outcome: Corrections to price, stock, descriptions, or images are applied without re-creating the listing

businessSolution: Temporarily hiding products (rather than deleting them) preserves all data while keeping them invisible to customers. This is ideal for seasonal items, out-of-stock situations, or products awaiting updated images.
navigation: Product Management → Product List → Toggle Status switch to Enable/Disable
feature: Enable or disable a product using the status toggle
outcome: Out-of-season or out-of-stock products are hidden from customers without permanent deletion

businessSolution: Permanent deletion frees up catalogue space by removing truly discontinued items. Since this action is irreversible, use the status toggle for temporary situations and delete only when you're certain.
navigation: Product Management → Product List → Click Delete icon → Confirm (irreversible action)
feature: Delete a product permanently from the catalogue
outcome: Discontinued SKUs are removed cleanly, freeing up catalogue space
setupComments: Action is irreversible

businessSolution: Running out of stock means lost sales and disappointed customers. Daily low-stock monitoring allows you to contact suppliers proactively and reorder before items become unavailable on the storefront.
navigation: Product Management → Low Stock → Review the filtered list of products below reorder threshold
feature: View a filtered list of all products whose stock quantity is at or below the low-stock threshold
outcome: I can prioritise reordering before products go out of stock and cause lost sales

businessSolution: When a supplier delivery arrives, immediately updating the stock count ensures your storefront reflects actual availability. This prevents the 'out of stock' message that drives customers to competitors.
navigation: Product Management → Low Stock → Click item → Enter new stock quantity → Save
feature: Update the stock quantity of a low-stock product directly from the low-stock list
outcome: I can top up inventory without navigating through the full product editor

businessSolution: Customer reviews are a direct signal of product quality and satisfaction. Patterns in reviews (e.g., multiple mentions of 'wrong size') indicate systemic issues that need operational attention.
navigation: Product Management → Reviews → View all reviews with ratings, comments, and reviewer names
feature: View all customer reviews submitted for products, including rating, comment, and reviewer name
outcome: I have visibility over product satisfaction across the entire catalogue

businessSolution: Fake, abusive, or spam reviews damage product credibility and mislead other customers. Prompt removal protects the integrity of your ratings system and maintains customer trust.
navigation: Product Management → Reviews → Click Delete on the inappropriate review → Confirm
feature: Delete an inappropriate or fraudulent product review
outcome: Fake or abusive reviews are removed before they damage product reputation

businessSolution: Having a pre-filled template with existing product data lets you add new products alongside current ones. The correct format is guaranteed, eliminating import errors from mismatched columns.
navigation: Product Management → Bulk Import → Download Template (pre-filled with existing data)
feature: Download the product bulk-import Excel template pre-filled with existing product data
outcome: I can use the exported file as a base for updates rather than starting from scratch

businessSolution: Mass product onboarding is critical when launching with a new supplier. Instead of creating each product manually, bulk import processes hundreds of SKUs in minutes with consistent data quality.
navigation: Product Management → Bulk Import → Choose filled .xls/.xlsx file → Upload
feature: Import a filled Excel file to add hundreds of new products in a single operation
outcome: Mass product onboarding is completed in minutes with zero manual form filling
setupComments: Only .xls/.xlsx accepted; all mandatory columns must be filled

businessSolution: Regular catalogue exports serve multiple purposes: supplier negotiations (sharing current assortment), inventory audits (offline review), accounting (product-level reporting), and disaster recovery (backup).
navigation: Product Management → Bulk Export → Click Export → Download Excel file
feature: Export the full product catalogue to Excel for reporting, backup, or external sharing
outcome: I always have an up-to-date offline copy of the product database for compliance or handover

businessSolution: Operational zones define where your platform provides services. Precisely drawn boundaries ensure that only customers within your delivery radius see available stores, preventing orders you can't fulfil.
navigation: Zone Setup → Add New → Draw boundary polygon on Google Map → Enter zone name in all languages → Save
feature: Draw a new operational zone boundary on the interactive Google Map and name it in multiple languages
outcome: A precise geographic service area is defined so stores and deliveries can be restricted to that region
setupComments: Both polygon drawing and zone name are mandatory

businessSolution: A clear overview of all operational zones helps assess geographic coverage. Exporting the list supports management review of expansion strategy and identifies potential coverage gaps.
navigation: Zone Setup → View list → Search by name → Click Export for spreadsheet download
feature: View, search, and export all existing zones in the zone management list
outcome: I have a clear overview of every service region and can audit them at any time

businessSolution: Emergency suspension of a zone (due to flooding, construction, or safety issues) instantly removes all services in that area. Customers see no stores rather than placing undeliverable orders.
navigation: Zone Setup → Toggle zone Status to Disable (re-enable when area is accessible)
feature: Enable or disable an entire zone using the master status toggle
outcome: All storefronts, deliveries, and customer access in a problematic region can be suspended instantly
setupComments: Disabling a zone removes it from the customer app in real time

businessSolution: A zone without a connected business module offers nothing to customers. Connecting a module (e.g., eCommerce, Grocery) activates that type of service within the geographic boundary.
navigation: Zone Setup → Click grid action icon next to zone → Select business module → Connect
feature: Connect a business module to a zone using the grid action icon
outcome: The zone starts offering the selected type of services (e.g., eCommerce) to customers in that area
setupComments: A zone without a connected module offers no services

businessSolution: As cities expand or delivery capabilities change, zone boundaries need updating. Adjusting the polygon ensures your service area matches current reality without recreating the entire zone.
navigation: Zone Setup → Click Edit on zone → Drag polygon points on map to new boundary → Save
feature: Edit the drawn boundary polygon of an existing zone
outcome: Service area adjustments are reflected immediately without recreating the zone from scratch

businessSolution: Each business module represents a vertical (Grocery, Food, Pharmacy). Creating a new module opens an entirely new line of business on your platform, attracting both relevant stores and customers.
navigation: Module Setup → Create New → Select type → Enter name, description → Upload icon + thumbnail → Save
feature: Create a new business module type (Grocery, Food, Pharmacy, eCommerce, Parcel) with name, description, icon, and thumbnail
outcome: A new vertical is available for stores to register under and for customers to browse on the home screen
setupComments: Module type cannot be changed after creation

businessSolution: As your platform scales to many business verticals, efficient filtering and searching helps you manage modules without confusion. Filtering by type shows only the relevant vertical.
navigation: Module Setup → Filter by module type → Use search bar to find by name
feature: Filter the module list by module type and search by name
outcome: I can locate specific modules instantly as the platform scales to many business verticals

businessSolution: Disabling a module is useful for soft launches (set up everything, test internally, then enable for customers) or temporary suspensions during platform maintenance or business restructuring.
navigation: Module Setup → Toggle Status to Enable/Disable
feature: Enable or disable a business module to control its visibility on the customer app home screen
outcome: A module under maintenance or review is hidden until it is ready
setupComments: Disabling hides all stores under this module

businessSolution: As your business evolves, module branding and descriptions may need updates. Changing the icon, name, or description refreshes the customer's home screen without affecting registered stores.
navigation: Module Setup → Click Edit → Update name, description, icon, or thumbnail → Save
feature: Edit a module's name, description, icon, or thumbnail after initial creation
outcome: Branding and descriptions stay accurate as the business evolves without recreating the module

businessSolution: Your company identity appears on receipts, emails, and as the default map centre for new customers. Correct setup ensures professional branding and accurate location-based experiences from day one.
navigation: Business Settings → Business Information → Fill company name, email, phone, country, address, map coordinates → Save
feature: Set global company name, email, phone, country, address, and map coordinates as platform defaults
outcome: All receipts, emails, and the customer app default map reflect the correct company identity
setupComments: Latitude and longitude set the default map centre for new users

businessSolution: Your logo and favicon represent your brand across all touchpoints — admin dashboard, customer web portal, and browser tabs. Consistent branding builds recognition and trust with every interaction.
navigation: Business Settings → Business Information → Upload Logo (3:1 ratio) → Upload Favicon (1:1 ratio) → Save
feature: Upload the platform logo (3:1 ratio) and favicon (1:1 ratio)
outcome: The brand identity is consistently displayed across the admin dashboard, web portal, and browser tabs

businessSolution: Locale settings affect how every financial figure, timestamp, and discount window appears across the platform. Incorrect settings can mislead customers on pricing or order deadlines.
navigation: Business Settings → Business Information → Set time zone, time format, currency symbol, currency position, decimal precision → Save
feature: Configure time zone, time format, currency symbol, currency position, and decimal precision
outcome: All financial figures, order timestamps, and discount windows reflect the correct local standards
setupComments: Currency and decimal changes affect all displayed prices immediately

businessSolution: Maintenance mode provides a controlled shutdown for planned updates. Customers see a maintenance screen instead of encountering errors, while admins continue working on the backend.
navigation: Business Settings → Business Information → Toggle Maintenance Mode ON (disable when done)
feature: Activate Maintenance Mode to disable all customer-facing apps and APIs during critical updates
outcome: Customers see a maintenance screen and cannot place orders during planned downtime, protecting data integrity
setupComments: Only Admin panel remains accessible in maintenance mode

businessSolution: Self-registration enables organic marketplace growth — any interested vendor can apply to join. Disabling it creates a curated, invite-only marketplace where you control vendor quality from the start.
navigation: Business Settings → Vendor Settings → Toggle 'Vendor Self-Registration' Enable/Disable
feature: Enable or disable vendor self-registration so external users can apply to become store owners
outcome: I control whether the vendor network grows organically or only through admin invitation

businessSolution: Product approval ensures nothing reaches customers without admin review. This is your quality gate — preventing low-quality images, incorrect pricing, or policy-violating products from going live.
navigation: Business Settings → Vendor Settings → Enable 'Need Approval For Products' → Select scope (Add/Price/Details)
feature: Enable product approval workflow so every new vendor product requires admin review before going live
outcome: Platform quality is maintained by preventing unapproved or low-quality products from reaching customers
setupComments: Can be applied for: Add new product, Update price, Update details

businessSolution: Controlling vendor cancellation rights enforces fulfilment commitments. Allowing cancellation gives vendors flexibility; disabling it ensures customers aren't disappointed by last-minute vendor-side rejections.
navigation: Business Settings → Vendor Settings → Toggle 'Allow Vendor Order Cancellation' Enable/Disable
feature: Allow or prevent vendors from cancelling orders
outcome: Order fulfilment policies are enforced consistently, reducing customer disappointment

businessSolution: Vendor replies to reviews show customers that sellers care about feedback. This transparency builds trust and can turn a negative review into a positive customer experience.
navigation: Business Settings → Vendor Settings → Toggle 'Allow Vendor Review Replies' Enable/Disable
feature: Allow vendors to publicly reply to customer product reviews
outcome: Vendor engagement with feedback boosts customer trust and platform transparency

businessSolution: Cash-on-delivery creates cash flow risk when vendors hold too much unremitted cash. Automatic thresholds restrict new COD orders until the cash is remitted, protecting your finances.
navigation: Business Settings → Vendor Settings → Set Cash-In-Hand maximum amount and minimum repayment → Save
feature: Configure Cash-In-Hand Overflow thresholds to restrict vendors holding too much unremitted cash
outcome: Cash flow risks from COD are managed automatically without manual intervention
setupComments: Set maximum amount and minimum repayment values

businessSolution: Overloaded drivers deliver late, damaging customer satisfaction. Setting a sensible maximum ensures each delivery gets proper attention and arrives on time.
navigation: Business Settings → Deliveryman Settings → Set maximum simultaneous orders per driver → Save
feature: Set the maximum number of orders a single delivery driver can carry simultaneously
outcome: No driver can accept more jobs than they can handle, ensuring on-time deliveries

businessSolution: Photo proof eliminates delivery disputes entirely. Every completed delivery has a timestamped image proving it was left at the right location — no more he-said-she-said situations.
navigation: Business Settings → Deliveryman Settings → Toggle 'Photo Proof of Delivery' Enable
feature: Enable photo proof of delivery, requiring drivers to capture an image before marking orders as Delivered
outcome: Disputes about non-delivery are resolved with photographic evidence in every case

businessSolution: Driver tipping improves fleet morale and retention at no additional platform cost. Happy drivers deliver better service, creating a positive cycle of customer satisfaction.
navigation: Business Settings → Deliveryman Settings → Toggle 'Customer Tipping' Enable
feature: Enable the customer tipping feature for delivery drivers during checkout
outcome: Drivers earn additional gratuity, improving fleet retention and morale

businessSolution: Self-registration grows your delivery fleet quickly but with less control. Admin-only registration ensures every driver is vetted, background-checked, and trained before their first delivery.
navigation: Business Settings → Deliveryman Settings → Toggle 'Driver Self-Registration' Enable/Disable
feature: Allow or prevent drivers from registering on the platform independently
outcome: I control the fleet size and vet drivers before they join the delivery network

businessSolution: The wallet ecosystem keeps refund money within your platform. Instead of refunds going back to bank accounts, the balance stays as wallet credit, encouraging customers to make another purchase.
navigation: Business Settings → Customer & Wallet → Toggle 'Customer Wallet' Enable
feature: Enable the customer wallet feature so users can store balance and receive refunds in-app
outcome: Refunds and pre-loaded funds remain within the platform, improving reinvestment rates

businessSolution: Loyalty points create a gamified rewards system that drives repeat purchases without the overhead of manual coupon management. Customers feel they're earning value with every order.
navigation: Business Settings → Customer & Wallet → Set earning %, conversion rate, minimum redemption threshold → Save
feature: Configure loyalty points: earning percentage per order, point-to-currency conversion rate, and minimum redemption threshold
outcome: A gamified rewards system boosts repeat purchases without manual coupon management
setupComments: Example: 2% earn rate, 100 points = 1 BHD, minimum 500 points to redeem

businessSolution: Referral programs turn your existing customers into brand ambassadors. Each successful referral brings a new customer at a fraction of traditional marketing cost — viral growth powered by your platform.
navigation: Business Settings → Customer & Wallet → Toggle 'Referral Program' Enable
feature: Enable the referral program so existing customers earn rewards for inviting new users
outcome: Customer acquisition is driven virally through word-of-mouth at low marketing cost

businessSolution: The global refund toggle gives you a kill switch for the entire refund process. Useful during peak seasons when your team is overwhelmed, or when investigating potential refund abuse.
navigation: Business Settings → Refund Settings → Toggle 'Refund Requests' Enable/Disable
feature: Enable or disable the global refund request feature for customers
outcome: I can open or freeze the refund system depending on operational capacity

businessSolution: The refund eligibility window (e.g., 7 days) creates a fair balance between customer protection and business sustainability. After the window closes, orders are considered final.
navigation: Business Settings → Refund Settings → Set refund window in days → Save
feature: Set the refund eligibility window in days after a delivery is completed
outcome: Only orders within the allowed time window can request refunds, preventing abuse

businessSolution: Structured refund reasons turn raw complaints into categorised data. Analysing whether most refunds are 'Damaged item' vs 'Wrong order' tells you whether the problem is in warehouse packing or order processing.
navigation: Business Settings → Refund Settings → Add/Edit/Delete refund reason options
feature: Add, edit, or delete refund reason options shown to customers during the refund flow
outcome: Refund data is categorised accurately for analytics and dispute resolution
setupComments: Examples: Damaged item, Missing item, Wrong order

businessSolution: Adding a new platform language unlocks an entire market segment. RTL support for Arabic/Hebrew automatically flips the entire UI layout, making the experience feel native to those users.
navigation: Business Settings → Language Setup → Add language → Enter ISO code → Select LTR or RTL → Save
feature: Add a new platform language specifying the ISO language code and text direction (LTR or RTL)
outcome: The platform becomes accessible to a new language market immediately
setupComments: RTL setting flips the entire app UI for Arabic/Hebrew

businessSolution: The default language determines the first impression for every new customer. Choose the most widely spoken language in your primary market to maximise initial engagement.
navigation: Business Settings → Language Setup → Click 'Set as Default' on the preferred language
feature: Set a language as the default for new customer sessions
outcome: All first-time visitors see the platform in the most appropriate language for the market

businessSolution: Tax compliance is a legal requirement. Every tax type you create (e.g., VAT 5%) appears on customer invoices, demonstrating transparent, regulation-compliant pricing.
navigation: Business Settings → System Tax → Create New → Enter tax name and percentage rate → Save
feature: Create a new tax type with a name and percentage rate (e.g., VAT 5%)
outcome: Taxes are available for assignment to orders or products, ensuring regulatory compliance
setupComments: Tax name appears on customer invoices

businessSolution: Inclusive vs exclusive tax affects how customers perceive prices. Some markets require prices to include tax; others add it at checkout. Check local regulations to determine the correct model.
navigation: Business Settings → System Tax → Select Inclusive or Exclusive tax calculation method → Save
feature: Configure whether tax is calculated inclusive (baked into price) or exclusive (added on top) of product prices
outcome: Customers see the correct pricing model expected by local consumer protection regulations

businessSolution: Subscription tiers create a scalable revenue model. Basic plans attract small vendors to try the platform; premium plans monetise features like POS integration, self-delivery, and advanced analytics.
navigation: Business Settings → Subscription Management → Create New → Set name, price, validity, feature checklist → Save
feature: Create subscription packages with name, price, validity period, and available features checklist
outcome: Vendors are offered tiered plans that gate features based on their subscription level
setupComments: Features: POS, self-delivery, app access, reviews, chat

businessSolution: Operational limits per tier (max products, max orders) create natural upgrade pressure. As vendors grow their business, hitting limits incentivises them to move to a higher-paying plan.
navigation: Business Settings → Subscription Management → Edit package → Set max order and product limits → Save
feature: Set maximum order and product limits per subscription package
outcome: Vendors are incentivised to upgrade their plan when they hit operational limits

businessSolution: Free trials eliminate the financial barrier to trying your platform. Vendors can evaluate features risk-free, increasing sign-up rates and building confidence before committing to a paid plan.
navigation: Business Settings → Subscription Management → Edit/Create package → Set free trial period → Save
feature: Configure a free trial period so new vendors can evaluate the platform before committing to a paid plan
outcome: Vendor acquisition is easier with a risk-free onboarding pathway

businessSolution: Legal pages (Terms, Privacy Policy, Refund Policy) are often legally required and build customer trust. Keeping them current protects your business from legal exposure and shows professionalism.
navigation: Business Settings → Pages & Social → Select page → Edit in rich-text editor → Save
feature: Edit the Terms & Conditions, Privacy Policy, Refund Policy, Cancellation Policy, and About Us pages using the rich-text editor
outcome: Legal and informational pages are always up to date and visible to customers
setupComments: All pages support multilingual content

businessSolution: Social media links extend your brand presence beyond the platform. Customers who follow you on social channels are more engaged and more likely to return for repeat purchases.
navigation: Business Settings → Pages & Social → Enter Facebook/Instagram/Twitter/LinkedIn URLs → Save
feature: Add or update social media links (Facebook, Instagram, Twitter, LinkedIn) shown in the platform footer
outcome: Customers can follow the brand on their preferred social channel directly from the app or website

businessSolution: A centralised gallery prevents duplicate uploads and makes visual assets easy to find and reuse across the entire platform — from product listings to banners to store pages.
navigation: Business Settings → Gallery → Browse folders (Products, Store, Banner, Category)
feature: Browse the centralised gallery file explorer organised into system folders (Products, Store, Banner, Category, etc.)
outcome: I can locate any uploaded file quickly without searching through individual sections

businessSolution: Batch uploading via zip archives drastically speeds up media onboarding. One zip with 100 images is uploaded and extracted in seconds instead of uploading each file individually.
navigation: Business Settings → Gallery → Click Upload → Select individual images or zip archive → Upload
feature: Upload individual images or a zip archive of multiple images to the gallery
outcome: New visual assets are stored centrally and available to reuse across the entire platform
setupComments: Zip upload auto-extracts all contained images

businessSolution: Full asset management (preview, copy URL, download, delete) gives you complete control. Copying direct URLs lets you embed gallery images in external tools, emails, or social media posts.
navigation: Business Settings → Gallery → Select file → Click Preview/Copy URL/Download/Delete
feature: Preview, copy the direct URL, download, or delete any file in the gallery
outcome: Asset management is efficient and I can share or embed gallery URLs in external tools
setupComments: Delete action is permanent and irreversible

businessSolution: Websocket configuration powers all real-time features — live driver tracking on the map and instant push notifications. Incorrect settings break these features for every user simultaneously.
navigation: Business Settings → Websocket Config → Enter server URL and port → Save (with DevOps guidance only)
feature: Configure the websocket server URL and port for real-time order tracking and push notifications
outcome: Live driver tracking on the map and instant alerts function correctly for all app users
setupComments: Only change with DevOps guidance; errors break live tracking

businessSolution: The landing page is often the first thing potential customers see. Keeping it aligned with current promotions and brand messaging maximises conversion from visitors to registered users.
navigation: Business Settings → Landing Page → Edit header, subtitle, hero banner, content blocks → Save
feature: Edit the header title, subtitle, hero banner image, and marketing content blocks on the customer web landing page
outcome: The public-facing web portal reflects current promotions and brand messaging accurately

businessSolution: Full real-time visibility into every transaction ensures no order is missed. This single view is your operations dashboard — catching a missed order early prevents customer complaints and cancellations.
navigation: Order Management → Order List → View all orders (ID, customer, total, status, date)
feature: View a real-time list of all orders showing order ID, customer name, total, status, and date
outcome: I have full visibility of every transaction in a single view without missing any incoming order

businessSolution: Status-based filtering lets you focus on what needs attention right now. Viewing only 'Pending' orders during peak hours ensures new orders get confirmed quickly before customers cancel.
navigation: Order Management → Order List → Click status tabs (Pending/Confirmed/Packed/Dispatched/Delivered/Cancelled/Returned)
feature: Filter orders by status (Pending, Confirmed, Packed, Dispatched, Delivered, Cancelled, Returned)
outcome: I see only the orders requiring action at each stage of the fulfilment workflow

businessSolution: Fast order lookup is essential for customer service. When a customer calls about their order, finding it within seconds demonstrates professionalism and resolves issues quickly.
navigation: Order Management → Order List → Search by order ID or customer name → Filter by date range
feature: Filter orders by date range and search by customer name or order ID
outcome: I can locate any specific order instantly for customer service or accounting purposes

businessSolution: Quick order confirmation builds customer trust. The faster you confirm, the less likely the customer is to second-guess their purchase or cancel out of uncertainty.
navigation: Order Management → Order List → Click Accept on pending order → Order moves to Confirmed
feature: Accept a new order with a single click to move it from Pending to Confirmed
outcome: The customer receives an instant confirmation notification and I commit to fulfilling the order

businessSolution: Transparent rejection with a reason is better than silence. Customers understand honest explanations (out of stock, delivery area issue) and are more likely to order again in the future.
navigation: Order Management → Order List → Click Reject → Select reason → Confirm
feature: Reject an incoming order and provide a reason (e.g., out of stock, cannot deliver to address)
outcome: The customer is informed promptly without waiting indefinitely, and the reason is logged for analysis

businessSolution: Each status update triggers a customer notification, reducing 'Where is my order?' enquiries. Consistent status progression shows operational maturity and builds customer confidence in your fulfilment process.
navigation: Order Management → Order Details → Update stage: Confirmed → Packed → Dispatched → Delivered
feature: Update the order stage sequentially: Confirmed → Packed → Dispatched → Delivered
outcome: Customers receive a live status update at every milestone, reducing customer service enquiries

businessSolution: Complete order details (line items, quantities, customer address, payment method) are everything you need for accurate fulfilment. Missing this information leads to wrong deliveries and costly corrections.
navigation: Order Management → Click order → View full detail page with all line items and customer info
feature: View the complete order detail page including all line items, quantities, customer address, and selected payment method
outcome: I have all information needed to fulfil the order correctly without contacting the customer

businessSolution: Professional paperwork (invoices, delivery notes) accompanies each delivery and serves as accounting documentation. Digital copies are available for reconciliation, tax filing, and dispute resolution.
navigation: Order Management → Order Details → Click 'Generate Invoice' or 'Delivery Note' → Download/Print
feature: Generate and download or print an invoice or delivery note for the order
outcome: Proper paperwork accompanies each delivery and is available for accounting reconciliation

businessSolution: A structured return process replaces ad hoc conversations with a trackable workflow. Both the customer and admin know the exact status of every return request at all times.
navigation: Order Management → Returns & Refunds → Click 'Initiate Return' on eligible order
feature: Initiate a return or refund for an eligible delivered order within the configured refund window
outcome: Customer disputes are handled through a structured process rather than ad hoc conversations
setupComments: Refund window is configured in Business Settings

businessSolution: Documented refund decisions (approved with reason, or denied with explanation) create an audit trail. Approved refunds automatically credit the customer's wallet, ensuring transparent financial handling.
navigation: Order Management → Returns & Refunds → Review request → Click Approve or Deny → Add admin notes
feature: Approve or deny a customer refund request and log the admin decision with notes
outcome: Refund decisions are documented and the customer wallet is updated automatically upon approval

businessSolution: Monitoring return volumes by status reveals operational patterns. A spike in returns for a specific product or store signals quality issues that need investigation before they damage your reputation.
navigation: Order Management → Returns & Refunds → Filter by status (Pending/Approved/Denied) → Review trends
feature: View the complete returns list filtered by status (Pending, Approved, Denied)
outcome: I can monitor the volume and nature of returns to identify product quality issues

businessSolution: The dashboard is your daily health-check. One glance at total orders, revenue, active stores, and customer count tells you whether the platform is trending up, flat, or needs immediate attention.
navigation: Reports & Analytics → Dashboard → View summary cards for current period
feature: View a summary dashboard showing total orders, revenue, active stores, and registered customers for the current period
outcome: I get an instant health-check of the platform without running individual reports

businessSolution: Revenue trend analysis reveals seasonal patterns and campaign effectiveness. Comparing this month vs last month, or this week vs same week last year, informs inventory and marketing decisions.
navigation: Reports & Analytics → Sales Report → Select date range (today/week/month/year/custom) → View
feature: Filter the sales report by a custom date range, or by preset periods (today, this week, this month, this year)
outcome: I can analyse revenue performance for any specific period to identify trends or seasonal patterns

businessSolution: Breaking revenue by module, store, or category reveals where income actually comes from. This data drives resource allocation — invest more in high-performing segments, investigate underperformers.
navigation: Reports & Analytics → Sales Report → Select breakdown by module/store/category → Analyse
feature: View revenue broken down by business module, store, or product category
outcome: I can identify which segments of the business are driving the most income and allocate resources accordingly

businessSolution: Top sellers deserve priority: keep them stocked, feature them in promotions, and ensure their listings are optimised. These products are your revenue engines — protecting their availability is critical.
navigation: Reports & Analytics → Product Report → View top-selling products ranked by quantity and revenue
feature: View a top-selling products report ranked by quantity sold and revenue generated
outcome: I can prioritise restocking and marketing for my best performers

businessSolution: Zero-sales products tie up catalogue space and may confuse customers. Decide whether to promote them (better images, discounts), rewrite descriptions, or discontinue them to focus on winners.
navigation: Reports & Analytics → Product Report → View low-performing/zero-sales product report
feature: View a low-performing or zero-sales product report
outcome: I can make informed decisions about discontinuing or promoting underperforming SKUs

businessSolution: Customer acquisition data shows whether your growth engine is working. Spikes after marketing campaigns confirm ROI; flat periods signal the need for new strategies or channels.
navigation: Reports & Analytics → Customer Report → View new registration trends over time
feature: View customer acquisition data showing new registrations over time
outcome: I understand growth trends and the effectiveness of marketing campaigns

businessSolution: Repeat customers have the highest lifetime value and lowest acquisition cost. Identifying and nurturing this segment with loyalty rewards or exclusive offers maximises long-term revenue.
navigation: Reports & Analytics → Customer Report → View repeat purchase rates → Identify loyal segments
feature: View repeat purchase rates and identify loyal customer segments
outcome: I can target loyalty campaigns at customers with high lifetime value

businessSolution: Store-level performance comparison identifies which stores need support. High cancellation rates may indicate stock issues; low revenue may indicate poor product assortment or pricing.
navigation: Reports & Analytics → Store Report → View per-store orders, revenue, cancellation rate
feature: View per-store performance metrics including orders completed, revenue, and cancellation rate
outcome: Underperforming stores are identified early and can be supported or reviewed

businessSolution: Delivery speed and reliability directly impact customer satisfaction and reorder rates. Identifying slow zones or underperforming drivers helps you optimise routing and fleet management.
navigation: Reports & Analytics → Delivery Report → View average time, on-time rate, driver scores
feature: View delivery performance metrics including average delivery time, on-time rate, and driver performance scores
outcome: Fleet efficiency issues are identified and driver training or routing can be improved

businessSolution: A complete financial audit trail is essential for regulatory compliance and bank reconciliation. Every monetary movement — payments, refunds, commissions — is timestamped and traceable.
navigation: Reports & Analytics → Transaction Report → View payments, refunds, wallet credits, commissions
feature: View a full financial transaction log including payments, refunds, wallet credits, and commissions
outcome: Every monetary movement is auditable and reconcilable against bank records

businessSolution: Exporting reports in standard formats (Excel, CSV) makes data accessible to accountants, partners, and management. These exports power presentations, tax filings, and compliance documentation.
navigation: Reports & Analytics → Any report → Click Export → Choose Excel or CSV → Download
feature: Export any report view as an Excel (.xlsx) or CSV file for offline analysis or sharing
outcome: Reports are available in standard formats for accountants, partners, or management decks

businessSolution: Knowing your exact financial position — available balance, pending clearance, and withdrawable amount — enables better cash flow planning without constantly checking your bank account.
navigation: Wallet Management → Balance & Overview → View current balance, pending, and withdrawable amounts
feature: View my current wallet balance, amount pending clearance, and total amount available for withdrawal
outcome: I always know my exact financial position without checking my bank account

businessSolution: Withdrawing platform earnings to your bank account should be simple and fast. Once your payment method is saved, requesting a withdrawal takes seconds and enters the admin processing queue.
navigation: Wallet Management → Withdraw → Enter amount → Select payment method → Submit request
feature: Submit a withdrawal request specifying the amount and the registered payment method (bank transfer, mobile money, etc.)
outcome: I can transfer my earned platform balance to my real bank account when needed
setupComments: Available withdrawal methods are configured by admin

businessSolution: Withdrawal status tracking eliminates the anxiety of waiting for payouts. Seeing 'Approved' or 'Processing' gives you confidence without needing to contact support for updates.
navigation: Wallet Management → Withdrawal History → View status (Pending/Approved/Denied)
feature: Track the status of my withdrawal request (Pending, Approved, Denied)
outcome: I know whether my payout has been processed without contacting support

businessSolution: A complete transaction history provides full financial traceability. Every credit and debit is documented, enabling dispute resolution and accurate bookkeeping for tax purposes.
navigation: Wallet Management → Transaction History → View paginated list with date, amount, type, reference
feature: View a paginated transaction history listing every credit and debit with date, amount, type, and reference
outcome: I can reconcile all wallet movements and dispute any discrepancy with clear evidence

businessSolution: Filtering by transaction type isolates specific financial movements for focused review. During tax preparation, you might only need 'Commission' transactions; during a dispute, only 'Refund deductions'.
navigation: Wallet Management → Transaction History → Filter by type (Order/Refund/Commission/Adjustment)
feature: Filter transaction history by type (Order credit, Refund deduction, Commission, Manual adjustment)
outcome: I can quickly isolate specific transaction types for financial review or tax preparation

businessSolution: Automatic wallet credits upon delivery completion eliminate manual invoicing and billing follow-ups. Earnings are settled instantly in the background, creating a frictionless vendor experience.
navigation: Automatic — wallet is credited when driver marks order as Delivered (no manual action needed)
feature: Receive an automatic wallet credit when an order assigned to me is marked as Delivered by the driver
outcome: Earnings are settled without manual intervention, eliminating delays and billing disputes
setupComments: Settlement timing is configured in Business Settings

businessSolution: Transparent refund deductions build trust. When a return is approved, the deducted amount and reason are clearly visible in your transaction history, so there are no surprise balance changes.
navigation: Automatic — deduction appears in Transaction History when admin approves a customer return
feature: See refund deductions from my wallet when an admin approves a customer return for my order
outcome: The financial impact of returns is shown transparently so I can manage cash flow

businessSolution: Saving your preferred withdrawal method (bank account, IBAN, mobile wallet) once means every future withdrawal routes correctly without re-entering details. This reduces errors and speeds up payouts.
navigation: Wallet Management → Payment Methods → Add bank/IBAN/mobile wallet details → Save
feature: Add a preferred withdrawal method (bank account details, IBAN, mobile wallet number) to my profile
outcome: Withdrawal requests always route to the correct account without re-entering details each time
setupComments: Available method types are set by the platform admin

businessSolution: Advertising your store or products to targeted shoppers increases visibility and drives sales. Choosing the right format — banner, product spotlight, or featured store — depends on your marketing objective.
navigation: Advertisement → New Campaign → Select ad format (banner/product spotlight/featured store)
feature: Create a new advertisement campaign by selecting an ad format (banner, product spotlight, featured store)
outcome: My products or store are promoted to targeted shoppers through the most appropriate ad placement

businessSolution: Targeted advertising reduces wasted spend. Showing your grocery ad only to food-interested customers in your delivery zone maximises relevance and conversion rates.
navigation: Advertisement → Campaign Settings → Select zone, module, customer segment → Save
feature: Configure campaign target audience settings (zone, module, customer segment)
outcome: The ad reaches only the shoppers most likely to convert in my specific service area

businessSolution: Budget controls prevent advertising overspend. Daily caps ensure you never exceed your daily marketing allocation, while total budget limits cap the entire campaign investment.
navigation: Advertisement → Budget & Schedule → Set daily cap and/or total campaign budget → Save
feature: Set a daily spending cap and/or a total campaign budget limit
outcome: My advertising spend stays within the approved budget without manual monitoring

businessSolution: Scheduling campaigns for specific dates and times ensures promotions align with peak shopping periods. Automatic activation and deactivation eliminates the need for manual monitoring.
navigation: Advertisement → Budget & Schedule → Set start date/time and end date/time → Save
feature: Schedule campaign start and end dates and times
outcome: Promotions run automatically during peak periods without manual activation or deactivation

businessSolution: Pausing a campaign during slow business periods conserves budget. All settings, targeting, and performance data are preserved — resume instantly when conditions improve.
navigation: Advertisement → Campaign List → Click Pause on running campaign
feature: Pause a running campaign temporarily without losing its settings or progress
outcome: I can halt spending during slow periods and resume instantly when activity picks up

businessSolution: Stopping a campaign permanently ends spending while preserving all performance data. This archived data is valuable for analysing what worked when planning future promotions.
navigation: Advertisement → Campaign List → Click Stop → Campaign is archived with performance data
feature: Stop a campaign permanently and archive its performance data
outcome: Completed campaigns are stored for reference when planning future promotions

businessSolution: Campaign performance metrics (impressions, clicks, CTR, conversions) tell you whether your ad spend is generating returns. Low CTR means the creative needs improvement; low conversions means the landing experience needs work.
navigation: Advertisement → Performance Analytics → View impressions, clicks, CTR, conversions per campaign
feature: View impressions, clicks, click-through rate (CTR), and conversion count for each campaign
outcome: I can evaluate which ad formats and targeting settings deliver the best return on investment

businessSolution: Side-by-side campaign comparison reveals which format, targeting, or schedule delivers the best ROI. Use these insights to double down on what works and eliminate what doesn't.
navigation: Advertisement → Performance Analytics → Compare multiple campaigns in single report view
feature: Compare performance across multiple campaigns in a single report view
outcome: I can determine the most cost-effective advertising strategy by comparing campaign results side by side

businessSolution: Role-based access ensures team members only see what they need. Inviting employees with specific roles prevents security risks from shared owner accounts and limits accidental data changes.
navigation: Employee Management → Invite → Enter email → Select role → Send invitation
feature: Invite a new staff member by entering their email address and selecting a predefined role
outcome: Team members receive access credentials without me sharing my owner account login

businessSolution: Missed or expired invitation emails shouldn't block employee onboarding. Resending generates a fresh link without creating duplicate accounts in the system.
navigation: Employee Management → Employee List → Find pending invite → Click Resend Invitation
feature: Resend an invitation email to an employee who has not yet accepted access
outcome: Onboarding is not blocked if the original invitation email was missed or expired

businessSolution: Custom roles provide granular access control tailored to each job function. An order processor doesn't need access to financial settings; a marketing manager doesn't need to modify products.
navigation: Employee Management → Roles → Create New → Set module-level permissions (view/edit/delete/export) → Save
feature: Create a custom role with a specific combination of module-level permissions (view, edit, delete, export)
outcome: Each team member only accesses the sections relevant to their job, reducing security and error risk

businessSolution: Job responsibilities evolve, and permissions should evolve with them. Editing a role updates access for everyone assigned to it immediately — no need to modify each employee individually.
navigation: Employee Management → Roles → Click Edit → Add or remove permissions → Save
feature: Edit an existing role to add or remove permissions
outcome: Access levels evolve with job responsibilities without needing to recreate roles from scratch
setupComments: Changes apply immediately to all employees assigned the role

businessSolution: Unused roles clutter the permission system and risk accidental assignment. Deleting them keeps the role list clean — the system ensures no employees are still assigned before allowing it.
navigation: Employee Management → Roles → Click Delete (only allowed if no employees assigned)
feature: Delete a custom role that is no longer in use
outcome: Unused roles do not clutter the permission system or create accidental access grants
setupComments: Role must have no assigned employees before deletion

businessSolution: A clear overview of who has access, what role they're in, and when they last logged in helps identify security risks — inactive accounts, over-privileged roles, or unknown users.
navigation: Employee Management → Employee List → View name, email, role, last login date
feature: View a list of all active employees showing name, email, assigned role, and last login date
outcome: I have a clear at-a-glance picture of who has access and what they are authorised to do

businessSolution: In a large organisation, finding a specific team member for access review or role change should be instant. Search by name or email eliminates manual scrolling.
navigation: Employee Management → Employee List → Use search bar to find by name or email
feature: Search the employee list by name or email address
outcome: I can find a specific team member instantly in a large organisation

businessSolution: Immediate access revocation when an employee departs protects sensitive store and customer data. The account is disabled but records are preserved for future audit reference.
navigation: Employee Management → Employee List → Click Deactivate on departing employee's account
feature: Deactivate an employee's account immediately upon their departure
outcome: Platform access is revoked in real time, protecting sensitive store and customer data
setupComments: Deactivated accounts cannot log in but records are preserved

businessSolution: Reactivating a returning employee (contractor, re-hire) restores all their previous settings without creating a new account. Their profile, role, and activity history remain intact.
navigation: Employee Management → Employee List → Find deactivated account → Click Reactivate
feature: Reactivate a previously deactivated employee account
outcome: Returning contractors or re-hired staff are re-enabled without recreating their profile

businessSolution: Activity logs provide accountability and investigation capability. When data changes unexpectedly, the timestamped audit trail shows exactly who did what and when.
navigation: Employee Management → Activity Audit → View all employee actions with timestamps
feature: View an activity log of all actions performed by each employee (records created, orders modified, settings changed)
outcome: I can hold team members accountable and investigate any data discrepancy with a timestamped audit trail

businessSolution: Investigating a specific incident requires focused filtering — by date, employee, or action type. This precision saves hours compared to scrolling through an unfiltered log.
navigation: Employee Management → Activity Audit → Filter by date range, employee name, or action type
feature: Filter the activity log by date range, employee name, or action type
outcome: Auditing a specific incident is efficient without scrolling through unrelated historical entries

businessSolution: Every physical warehouse item needs a digital twin. SKU codes and barcodes enable scannable tracking; initial stock quantities set the baseline for all future inventory movements.
navigation: Item / Inventory → Item Creation → Enter SKU, barcode, unit of measure, initial stock → Save
feature: Add a new inventory item with SKU code, barcode, unit of measure, and initial stock quantity
outcome: Every physical item in my warehouse has a unique, trackable digital record

businessSolution: Low-stock thresholds are your early warning system. Setting them correctly gives you enough lead time to reorder from suppliers before items become unavailable and cause lost sales.
navigation: Item / Inventory → Item Creation/Edit → Set low-stock threshold value → Save
feature: Set a low-stock threshold for each item to trigger reorder alerts
outcome: Stock shortages are flagged before they result in lost sales or broken orders

businessSolution: Linking inventory items to storefront products creates automatic stock deduction. Every customer order reduces inventory without manual intervention, keeping your digital and physical stock in sync.
navigation: Item / Inventory → Item Linking → Select inventory item → Link to storefront product
feature: Link an inventory item directly to a product listing on the storefront
outcome: Every customer order automatically deducts the correct quantity from inventory without manual updates

businessSolution: After receiving supplier deliveries or completing physical counts, updating stock quantities keeps your digital inventory accurate. Mismatches lead to overselling or missed sales opportunities.
navigation: Item / Inventory → Item List → Click Edit on item → Update stock quantity → Save
feature: Manually update the stock quantity of a single item from the item edit screen
outcome: Inventory stays accurate after physical stock counts, deliveries from suppliers, or damage adjustments

businessSolution: Full warehouse counts produce hundreds of stock corrections. Bulk upload via CSV/Excel processes all corrections in one operation, eliminating hours of individual manual updates.
navigation: Item / Inventory → Bulk Stock Update → Prepare CSV/Excel with quantities → Upload
feature: Perform a bulk stock quantity update by uploading a pre-formatted CSV or Excel file
outcome: Hundreds of stock levels can be corrected after a physical warehouse count in a single operation

businessSolution: Automated low-stock notifications are your safety net against stock-outs. Acting on these alerts by contacting suppliers immediately prevents the 'out of stock' experience that drives customers to competitors.
navigation: Automatic — notifications received via in-app and/or email when stock falls below threshold
feature: Receive an in-app and/or email notification when any item's stock falls below its defined threshold
outcome: I replenish stock proactively and never encounter a situation where a popular item is unexpectedly unavailable
setupComments: Alert delivery channel configured in notification settings

businessSolution: Stock movement history provides complete auditability. Every in/out entry with date, reason, and quantity helps detect theft, data entry errors, or process breakdowns affecting inventory accuracy.
navigation: Item / Inventory → Stock Movement History → Select item → View all movement entries
feature: View a stock movement log for each item showing every in/out entry with date, reason, and quantity
outcome: I can trace exactly why stock levels changed and detect discrepancies caused by theft or data entry errors

businessSolution: Investigating specific discrepancies requires filtering by date range or transaction type. This precision pinpoints the exact event (order deduction, manual adjustment, or import) that caused the issue.
navigation: Item / Inventory → Stock Movement History → Filter by date range or transaction type
feature: Filter stock movement history by date range or transaction type (order deduction, manual adjustment, import)
outcome: Investigating a specific inventory discrepancy is fast and precise

businessSolution: Discount codes are versatile marketing tools — incentivising first purchases, rewarding loyalty, recovering abandoned carts, or driving traffic during slow periods. Each code ties directly to measurable results.
navigation: Marketing → Discount Codes → Create New → Set code, discount type (fixed/%), assign to products/store → Save
feature: Create a discount code with a fixed amount or percentage-off value and assign it to specific products or store-wide
outcome: I can incentivise first-time or returning customers with targeted offers

businessSolution: Usage controls protect your margins. Minimum order values ensure discounts only apply to profitable orders; per-customer limits prevent one person from exploiting the code repeatedly.
navigation: Marketing → Discount Codes → Edit → Set minimum order value + usage limits (total/per-customer) → Save
feature: Set a minimum order value and a usage limit (total uses and per-customer uses) on each discount code
outcome: Promotions remain profitable and cannot be exploited through mass redemptions by one customer

businessSolution: Time-limited offers create purchase urgency — customers buy now because they know the deal expires. Automatic expiry eliminates manual deactivation at midnight or on weekends.
navigation: Marketing → Discount Codes → Edit → Set expiry date and time → Save
feature: Set an expiry date and time for a discount code after which it becomes invalid at checkout
outcome: Time-limited offers create urgency without requiring manual deactivation

businessSolution: Monitoring discount code performance reveals which promotions drive real business. Codes with high usage and revenue lift should be repeated; underperforming codes should be deactivated to redirect marketing effort.
navigation: Marketing → Discount Codes → View list (usage count, remaining uses, expiry status)
feature: View the list of all discount codes with usage count, remaining uses, and expiry status
outcome: I can monitor code performance and deactivate ineffective codes early

businessSolution: Flash sales create exciting, time-limited shopping events that drive traffic and volume. Automated scheduling means the sale goes live and ends precisely when planned — even at midnight or during holidays.
navigation: Marketing → Flash Sales → Select products → Set discounted prices → Schedule start/end times → Save
feature: Schedule a flash sale on selected products specifying a discounted price and precise start/end date-time
outcome: Time-sensitive offers go live and expire automatically without any manual intervention at odd hours

businessSolution: Real-time control over flash sales lets you react to market conditions. Extend a sale that's driving exceptionally high traffic, or cancel one that's not performing — agility is key in promotions.
navigation: Marketing → Flash Sales → View active/scheduled sales → Click Extend or Cancel
feature: View all active and scheduled flash sales and cancel or extend individual sales
outcome: I maintain control over promotional pricing and can react quickly to changing business conditions

businessSolution: Bundles increase average order value by pairing complementary products at a discount. Customers feel they're getting a deal; you move slow-selling items alongside popular ones.
navigation: Marketing → Bundle Deals → Create New → Select products → Set bundle discount price → Save
feature: Create a bundle deal offering a discounted price when specific products are purchased together
outcome: Average order value increases as customers are incentivised to buy complementary products

businessSolution: Promotion ROI measurement is critical for marketing efficiency. A promotion that generates 100 orders but costs more in discounts than the incremental revenue it produces is actually losing money.
navigation: Marketing → Promotion Analytics → View redemption report (discount given, orders generated, revenue impact)
feature: View a redemption report for each promotion showing total discount given, number of orders generated, and revenue impact
outcome: I can directly measure the ROI of each promotion and remove ones that discount without driving incremental sales

businessSolution: A structured withdrawal queue ensures every vendor payout request is processed fairly and none fall through the cracks. Status filtering helps prioritise what needs immediate attention.
navigation: Financial Operations → Withdraw Requests → Filter by status (Pending/Approved/Denied)
feature: View all vendor withdrawal requests filtered by status (Pending, Approved, Denied)
outcome: Payout requests are processed in a structured queue without any falling through the cracks

businessSolution: Verifying payment details before approval prevents failed bank transfers and the administrative overhead of reprocessing. Each approval creates a logged reference for bank reconciliation.
navigation: Financial Operations → Withdraw Requests → Review details → Click Approve
feature: Approve a vendor withdrawal request after verifying payment method details
outcome: The vendor's balance is cleared and the payout is logged with a reference for reconciliation

businessSolution: Documented denial with admin notes creates a compliance trail. Vendors are notified with the reason, and the denial is on record in case of disputes or audits.
navigation: Financial Operations → Withdraw Requests → Click Deny → Add admin reason note → Confirm
feature: Deny a withdrawal request and provide an admin note explaining the reason
outcome: Problematic or fraudulent requests are rejected with a documented reason for compliance purposes

businessSolution: Full lifecycle tracking of store disbursements (Pending → Completed/Partially Completed/Cancelled) provides audit-ready documentation for regulatory compliance and financial reconciliation.
navigation: Financial Operations → Store Disbursement → View disbursement history with lifecycle stages
feature: View the full store disbursement history with lifecycle stages (Pending, Completed, Partially Completed, Cancelled)
outcome: Every payout batch is traceable from initiation to settlement for audit and compliance purposes

businessSolution: Batch processing of driver payouts with full lifecycle visibility ensures no driver is missed and every payment is traceable from initiation through completion.
navigation: Financial Operations → Delivery Disbursement → View and process driver payouts through lifecycle
feature: View and process delivery driver disbursements tracking the payment from Processing through Completed
outcome: Driver payouts are handled in batches with full lifecycle visibility

businessSolution: Every physical cash handover needs a digital record. Logging COD collections with amount, method, and reference creates a matching digital trail that eliminates gaps between cash and system balances.
navigation: Financial Operations → Collect Cash → Enter amount, payment method, reference number → Save
feature: Log a physical cash collection event from a delivery driver or store, specifying amount, payment method, and reference number
outcome: Every Cash-on-Delivery remittance is recorded digitally, eliminating any gap between physical and digital balances
setupComments: Required when COD is enabled on the platform

businessSolution: Regular cash reconciliation (physical cash held vs digital records) catches discrepancies early — whether from unlogged collections, recording errors, or potential mishandling.
navigation: Financial Operations → Collect Cash → View cash collection log → Reconcile against physical cash
feature: View the complete cash collection log to reconcile physical cash held against digital records
outcome: Discrepancies between physical cash and system balances are identified and resolved quickly

businessSolution: Configuring withdrawal methods with exact required fields ensures vendors and drivers provide precisely what's needed for payout processing. Incomplete details cause failed transfers and delays.
navigation: Financial Operations → Withdraw Methods → Add method (Bank/Mobile/PayPal) → Define required fields → Save
feature: Configure available withdrawal methods (Bank Transfer, Mobile Money, PayPal) and the custom fields required for each
outcome: Vendors and drivers provide the exact information needed for payout processing, reducing failed transfers
setupComments: Field examples: IBAN, Swift Code, Account Number, Mobile Number

businessSolution: Setting a default withdrawal method pre-selects the most common option, speeding up the process. Disabling outdated methods prevents selection of channels you no longer support.
navigation: Financial Operations → Withdraw Methods → Toggle Enable/Disable → Click 'Set as Default'
feature: Enable, disable, or set a withdrawal method as the default for new requests
outcome: Outdated payment channels are hidden and the most common method is pre-selected to speed up the process
